evals.prompt: My API endpoint that fetches a user's orders and their products is slow. Each order triggers separate product queries.
evals.expected_output: N+1 query detected. Fix: use JOIN or eager loading to batch-fetch products with orders in one query. Measure before/after with profiling. Add pagination (limit/offset). Implement caching for frequently accessed order data with appropriate TTL.

evals.prompt: Our React app has a 10-second initial load for a page with a large data table of 5000 rows.
evals.expected_output: Use virtualization (react-window or react-virtual) — render only visible rows. Implement lazy loading / pagination. Check for large bundle: use bundle-analyzer to find heavy deps. Apply code splitting with React.lazy. Wrap with Suspense for streaming. Target LCP < 2.5s.

evals.prompt: I want to preemptively optimize all functions before we see any performance issues.
evals.expected_output: Violation of performance engineering principle: No premature optimization. Profile first — measure CPU, memory, and latency baselines. Identify the actual top bottleneck (N+1, hot loop, memory leak). Fix only proven bottlenecks. Re-profile to confirm improvement and check for regressions. YAGNI applies to optimization too.

should_not_trigger: Write a README file for the project
